findings.issue: Task 07 declares depends_on: ["02", "05"], while the tech-spec Wave 3 entry names only Task 5 as a dependency. The added "02" is a justified extension (the verbose branch reads view.Verbose introduced by Task 2) and is explained in Description and Details, so this is a defensible carry-forward extension rather than a contradiction. Wave ordering holds (wave 2=1, wave 5=2, both < 3).
findings.fix: No change required. Optionally note in the tech-spec that Task 7 also depends on Task 2 for the verbose flag, to keep spec and task in lockstep.

notes.dependency_graph: Acyclic. 05->[02,04]; 07->[02,05]; 08->[01,03,05,07]; 09->[05,07,08]. All referenced IDs exist; every dependency sits in an earlier wave (waves: 01-04=1, 05=2, 07=3, 08=4, 09=5). No cycles.
notes.first_tick_mechanism: Consistent across 07/08/09. Task 07 introduces an explicit verboseFirstTick flag on Collector (set on first verbose collect, cleared after); Task 08 composers read the flag (not len(slice)==0) to render n/a, with an explicit counter-case (filled zero-delta slice without flag -> 0); Task 09 moves the flag into verboseCollectState and binds the 'collecting...' cmdline hint to it. All three repeat the same prev=curr-before-count rationale; no contradiction introduced by the edits.
notes.context_links: Context File links normalized to markdown [name](path) form with both name and path in all four tasks; no bare links or plain-text paths observed.
notes.carry_forward: Files-to-modify in each task match the tech-spec Implementation Tasks entry exactly (05: common.go/overview.go/postgres.go/stat.go; 07: stat.go; 08: top/stat.go/top/stat_test.go/fsstat.go; 09: stat.go/top/stat.go). AC and TDD anchors from the tech-spec descriptions are present and extended, none dropped.
notes.frontmatter: All four: status=planned, depends_on arrays, wave>=1, skills=[code-writing], reviewers arrays. skills and reviewers match the corresponding body sections. Tasks 05 and 08 include dev-security-auditor (security-sensitive: privileged aggregates / raw-path leakage); 07 and 09 omit it, consistent with the tech-spec.
notes.tdd_anchors: All present and behavior-checking (path::test_name -- description), including degradation, first-tick, and throttle/auto-resume behavior paths; no string-presence-only anchors.
